exemplar.title:
How effective does Refillable Cleaning Products by Clorox Company 
(TCC) improve environmental sustainability and increase their profitability?

detailedExemplarData.title:
How effective does Refillable Cleaning Products by Clorox Company 
(TCC) improve environmental sustainability and increase their profitability?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Correctly identifies and defines sustainability as the key concept in the introduction
Revisits sustainability in each analytical section (TBL, Ansoff, ratios, conclusion)
Discussion links sustainability to packaging reduction, Net-Zero goals, cost savings and consumer loyalty
weaknesses: References to sustainability become formulaic (e.g., repeated bolding without fresh insight)
Definition in the introduction is overly broad; lacks precise dimensions
Some financial comments drift away from the concept without reconnecting to sustainability
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although the student demonstrates a strong grasp of sustainability and applies it in multiple sections (initially assessed at band 4), integration is not fully seamless and occasionally feels formulaic. To reflect partial but not holistic integration throughout, a score of 3 is justified.

strengths: Precisely five supporting documents are cited, meeting the 3â5 requirement
Sources (investor reports, packaging industry articles, NY Times coverage) are relevant to cost figures and market context
weaknesses: Citation format is inconsistent and confusing (mixed footnote styles)
Key claims (e.g., 80% plastic savings, 9% reduction) lack inline citations or clear source links
âOther referencesâ list exceeds core sources, diluting focus
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Document count satisfies band 2, and the sources are generally relevant. However, inconsistent citation style, occasional missing references for key claims and an overlong supplementary list prevent a higher score, placing this criterion at 2.

strengths: Employs three appropriate tools (Triple Bottom Line, Ansoff Matrix, ratio analysis) covering environmental, strategic and financial perspectives
Each tool is applied to Cloroxâs refillable initiative with company-specific evidence
weaknesses: Application sometimes remains descriptive rather than analytical (e.g., listing product types)
Triple Bottom Line pillars are mislabelled, and empty headers in the Ansoff table confuse layout
Some numbers are unsubstantiated, weakening the link to the research question
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The studentâs choice and application of tools is adequate (originally band 3) but marred by layout issues and occasional descriptive passages. A holistic score of 2 accurately reflects this level of performance.
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Uses ratio table and cost-savings figures to support discussion
Links data to research question in multiple sections
weaknesses: Analysis is uneven and sometimes cursory, lacking counter-arguments or deeper evaluation
Key figures (e.g., R&D spend, cost savings) lack precise sourcing/context
Integration across sections is not consistently sustained
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While there is clear use of data and movement beyond pure description, the analysis is uneven and lacks sustained critical depth. This aligns with a mid-band performance, warranting a score of 2.

strengths: Conclusions are fully consistent with the evidence presented
The research question is explicitly answered and linked to analytical findings
weaknesses: Conclusion lacks a definitive closing statement that reaffirms the answer to the research question
Could synthesize insights more cohesively rather than summarising each tool separately
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusion fully answers the research question and aligns with evidence (band 3 potential), but it falls short of delivering a strong, unified closing. A balanced score of 2 reflects this.

strengths: Logical organization with title page, contents, numbered sections and clear sub-headings
Table of contents and consistent pagination aid navigation
weaknesses: Minor typographical errors and occasional awkward flow interrupt readability
Transitions between some sections could be smoother
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The report demonstrates an appropriate overall structure throughout (band 2). Minor language and flow issues do not undermine coherence, justifying full marks for this criterion.

strengths: All required IB elements are present (title, RQ, key concept, word count, citations, bibliography)
Visual formatting (headings, tables) is generally clear
weaknesses: Citation style is inconsistent (mix of formats and footnote placement)
Currency notation and numbering formats are sometimes incorrect or unclear
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All mandatory elements are included and presented clearly overall (band 2). Inconsistent formatting details prevent perfection but merit the full score for presentation.

strandSelections.reasoning: The report follows a logical structure: title page, table of contents, numbered sections, sub-headings and references, facilitating reader navigation.  Minor lapses (typographical errors, occasional awkward flow) do not undermine overall organisation, so it meets âappropriate structure throughoutâ (band 2).

The sources include investor reports, packaging industry articles and NY Times coverage that directly inform cost figures, ratio data and market context, indicating relevance.  Depth is adequate but not wide-ranging; most points still rely on secondary press releases rather than primary data or triangulation, so the evidence base is âsufficiently in-depthâ rather than âin-depth with rangeâ, placing it in band 3.
All required IB elementsâtitle, RQ, key concept, word count, contents page, citations and bibliographyâare present.  Therefore the work meets the highest presentation band (band 2).
Five numbered supporting documents are cited, satisfying the 3-5 requirement exactly.  Thus the work meets the criteria for band 2 on document count.

Each tool is applied to Cloroxâs refillable initiative: TBL links packaging changes to environmental benefits and costs, Ansoff positions refillables as product development, and ratios compare 2022 vs 2023 performance.  While links to the research question are generally clear, analysis sometimes becomes descriptive (e.g., listing product types) and contains unsubstantiated numbers, so effectiveness is âadequateâ rather than âclear and effectiveâ, warranting band 3.

The student presents a small but relevant data set (ratio table, cost savings, R&D spend).  Data underpin discussion in each section, showing adequate use, though figures are limited in scope and occasionally lack sourcing precision.  This fits the descriptor for band 3 âadequate selection and use of dataâ.

Analysis moves beyond descriptionâlinking reduced plastic to cost savings and margin changes, assessing risk within Ansoff and evaluating liquidity impacts.  Nonetheless, arguments are sometimes cursory, integration across sections is uneven, and counter-arguments are minimal, so the quality aligns with the mid-band (some effective analysis) rather than the higher bands that require sustained integration.
The research question is explicitly answered in the first sentence of the conclusion and supported by synthesis of the preceding analysis, satisfying the descriptor for an explicit answer (band 3).

The IA employs three appropriate tools (Triple Bottom Line, Ansoff Matrix, profitability & liquidity ratios).  This constitutes an adequateâthough not extensiveâselection covering strategic, ethical and financial perspectives, matching the descriptor for band 3.

The conclusion summarises findings from every analytical tool, states that refillables improve sustainability and set the firm up for future profitability, and these statements are in line with evidence presented (e.g., higher gross margin, TBL benefits).  No contradictions with data are noted, so conclusions are fully consistent, earning the top band for this strand.

Analysis is clearly rooted in The Clorox Company: cost figures, ratio table, R&D expenses, specific executives and product formats all demonstrate a strong organisational focus.  The tools (TBL, Ansoff, ratios) are applied using company-specific evidence rather than generic commentary, indicating sustained connection.  Nevertheless, a few claims (e.g., âannual cost â¦ 2â5 millionâ without citation, or vague reference to âAfricaâ) weaken full integration, so the strand sits just below the highest band.

The student explicitly identifies sustainability as the key concept, defines it in the introduction and revisits it in every analytical section (TBL, Ansoff, ratios, conclusion). Discussion links the concept to packaging reduction, Net-Zero goals, cost savings and consumer loyalty, showing a solid grasp of the environmental-economic trade-offs.  However, integration is not seamless throughout; some references to sustainability are formulaic (e.g., repeated bolded word without fresh insight) and certain financial comments drift away from the concept.  Overall this aligns with a âstrong understanding with partial integration,â hence band 4.

annotations.comment: Heading formatting in the table of contents appears inconsistent; ensure uniform font size and indentation for clarity.
The definition of sustainability in the introduction is overly broad; specify dimensions (environmental, social, economic) more precisely.
Citation format for footnotes is inconsistent; adopt a single citation style (APA, MLA) throughout to maintain professionalism.
The transition sentence ending âmore expensiveâ is awkward and lacks a concluding thought; revise for flow.
The research question is clearly stated and addresses both environmental and financial dimensions, facilitating focused analysis.
The key concept of âSustainabilityâ is correctly identified and highlighted, underpinning the investigation.
Consider refining the research question by specifying the timeframe for measuring âeffectivenessâ to improve focus and measurability.
The table of contents demonstrates a logical organization of sections and page numbering aids navigation.
The ratio analysis table lacks discussion of why profit margin declined from 6.5% to 2.02%; interpret these shifts.
Table headings âFiscal year 2022 ended 30â and âended 30â are truncated; revise to include full date (e.g., âended June 30, 2022â).
Discussion of current ratio changes is present but could link more explicitly to liquidity risks facing Clorox.
Currency notation (e.g., 5â12$) should precede the symbol (e.g., $5â$12) and specify USD to avoid ambiguity.
Repeated bolding of âsustainabilityâ in prose becomes formulaic; integrate fresh analysis to reinforce the conceptâs application.
The definition of Triple Bottom Line mislabels its three pillars; clarify as environmental, social, and economic.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Figure 1 caption is minimal; include concise interpretation of what the TBL analysis reveals about Cloroxâs initiatives.
The 9% plastic reduction claim lacks an inline citation; add a footnote to strengthen the evidence base.
Discussion of microplastic risks is persuasive but would benefit from quantitative data or a specific case study.
Cost figures (â2$ to 5$ millionâ) appear without context or footnote; specify the period and source.
In-text use of inline math tags (e.g., \text{year}^6) is not rendered; convert to proper LaTeX notation such as $$	ext{year}^6$$.
The Ansoff Matrix table uses empty header cells which confuse the layout; include clear labels for rows and columns.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Geographic regions (Africa, Asia Pacific, north America Europe) lack consistent capitalization and comma separation; revise for clarity.
Product descriptions on page 9 are lengthy; consider condensing to highlight strategic relevance rather than operational detail.
The 80% plastic savings claim requires direct citation; link to the supporting document inline.
Phrasing â2x times moreâ is redundant; use âtwice as manyâ or a precise multiplier.
Risk discussion on page 10 merges multiple footnotes (10,11) in one sentence, creating confusion; separate citations clearly.
Spelling mix between ânettâ and ânetâ income appears; standardize terminology in line with corporate reports.
Conclusion synthesizes tool insights effectively, but it lacks a definitive closing statement explicitly reaffirming the research questionâs answer.
References exhibit inconsistent formatting (e.g., italics, brackets); adopt a uniform style to enhance credibility.
Supporting documents list exceeds five items under âOther references,â which may dilute focus; ensure only 3â5 core sources.
Overall structure is logical with clear headings, but minor typographical errors occasionally disrupt flow.
The choice of Triple Bottom Line, Ansoff Matrix, and ratio analysis demonstrates adequate tool selection, covering strategic, environmental, and financial angles.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Analysis occasionally slips into description without critical evaluation; incorporate counterarguments or limitations to deepen analysis.
